series_description: Audits and reviews performed by an agency on external entities, on the agency by other entities, or on the agency by internal auditors. Includes working papers that support the audit.

comments:
Note: The State Auditor’s Office
(SAO) retains any copies of its
audits performed on Texas State
agencies. See Agency Item # (AIN)
TPW 5.4.018 and TPW 5.4.019 for
Audit Plan records.

series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by an agency.

electronically distributed by
Financial Resources.

Legislative Appropriation Requests

State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related

review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

another records series on the

Related Note: Correspondence
incorporated within another record

the designated retention period for
that series. Note: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Also see:
AIN TPW 1.1.007.B, 1.1.007.C and
1.1.007.D.

series_description: See 1.1.007.A description

Archivist, Archives & Info Services
Div., TSLAC.

retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year

purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee

subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General. Archives
Note: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records

and board or commission members

State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.

the State Records Retention
Schedule issued by TSLAC for
additional guidelines.
Note: The archival requirement is
met by sending the records to the
TPW Records Management Branch
for submission to the State
Archivist, Archives & Info Services
Div., TSLAC.

and Archival requirements would be

Texas Digital Archives, TSLAC.
Old Paper Records OR Items Not

(Sept.1), after they have fulfilled

Management Branch, for

Archives & Info. Services Div.,
TSLAC.

series_description: Information in any format that is publicly distributed and produced by the authority of or at the total or partial expense of a state agency or is required to be distributed under law by the agency. except a publication that is subject to a different retention period in this schedule. Includes but is not limited to: a publication distributed in print; on microform; as audiovisual material; as interactive media or on electronic external storage device; as an online publication (including websites); which is an index to other online publications; as one or more text, graphic, or other digital files; or as a user interface to a computer database. substantive drafts of major
publications may be archival. Major

authorship and its impact on Texas

negatives that have significant value

be archival.

State Publications created from
development files.

disciplinary action must be retained
for the minimum retention period
described by AIN TPW 3.1.021.

series_description: Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the agency, or the employee work force; and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self-improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.

Note: See related AIN TPW
5.3.007a/b/c for bid
documentation. See AIN TPW
5.2.028 for building construction
contracts. See AIN TPW 5.1.017 for
contract logs.

series_description: Contract records specifically used for agency concessioners.

review code removed subsequent

& Information Services Division,
10/18/99.

series_description: Any internally distributed manual, guidelines, or similar records that establish standard office procedures for an agency; for example, agency style manuals, telephone protocols, mail room procedures, standard photocopy instructions, imaging and conversion to electronic records plans.

Note: Also called Standard Office
Procedures; not to be confused

litigation.
Note: The disposal of surveillance
videos need not be documented
through destruction signoffs (AIN
TPW 1.2.001) or in records
disposition logs (AIN TPW 1.2.010).

series_description: Violation-related documents issued by Law Enforcement, State Park Police.

Estimate cost files are kept

information leads to request for

TPW 1.1.020 and 1.1.021 for public
information requests.

Note: may include documentation
and data entered into incident
reporting system or databases.

CAUTION: Does not include
inspection reports of building
construction.

individual employee training
records.

Lists (AIN TPW 5.4.009) are not

Data Sheets".

Notes: See AIN TPW 3.1.041 for

comments:
Note: See AIN TPW 5.4.017 for
records related to responding or
recovering from an emergency.

accident reports. See AIN TPW
5.4.001.
Note: may include documentation
and data entered into incident
reporting system or databases.

series_description: Reports of accidents to minors on state property or in any other situation in which the state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.

comments:
Note: may include documentation
and data entered into incident
reporting system or databases.

series_description: This series documents the use of hazardous carcinogenic compounds, building by building survey and plan to correct asbestos material hazards. This series may include but is not limited to: surveys; monitoring tests and reports; data forms; building plans; correction checklists; removal job records; and related documentation and correspondence; lists of carcinogenic compounds used; environmental, biological, and material safety monitoring reports concerning toxic substances and harmful physical agents in the workplace, including analyses derived from such reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years
comments:
Note: See AIN TPW 5.4.016.A/B for
hazardous material exposure
records.

series_description: Environmental, biological, and material safety monitoring reports, including health or physical examination reports or certificates of employees, who have experienced exposure to toxic substances, harmful physical agent, or bloodborne pathogens in the workplace, including analyses derived from such reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years

series_description: Environmental, biological, and material safety monitoring reports, including health or physical examination reports or certificates of employees, for whom periodic monitoring of health or fitness is required concerning toxic substances, harmful physical agent, or bloodborne pathogens in the workplace, including analyses derived from such reports.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 2 years

series_title: Emergency Response, Damage and Recovery Records
series_description: This series documents the events and damages to institutional property and assets due to storms, riots, fires, droughts, floods, and other events affecting citizens and facilities within the jurisdiction of the institution. This series may include but is not limited to: diaries; logs; reports; photographs; notes which indicate or document what happened, when, and where; and related documentation and correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If grant monies are
received to assist with the response
or recovery, the retention
requirements for this series may
vary depending on the specific
funding agency. Note: If any
litigation, claim, audit or other
action involving the records begins
prior to the expiration of the three-
year period, the records must be
maintained until resolution of all
issues or until the end of the three-
year period, whichever is later.
Note: See AIN TPW 5.4.013 for
Continuity of Operations Plans
(COOP).

series_description: Includes working papers and agency risk assessment used to develop the plan, per Texas Internal Auditing Act requirement.

Note: See AIN TPW 1.1.002 for
individual Audit records not related

series_description: Documents collected or generated as part of the process of reviewing other state agency internal audit programs. retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Note: See AIN TPW 1.1.002 for
individual Audit records not related
to the peer review of an Audit Plan.
